items.text:
And ever since then,
unethical use of transmutation
has been explicitly prohibited.

Or unauthorized use, generally.
No publicly-visible use
except in life-or-death situations.

There's a scroll called
The Constraints on the Sacred Art of Transmutation
("The Constraints" for short),

that we all must memorize and swear by,
and which lays out the restrictions in detail.

For example, we shall not transmute
a flowering bush or tree in the springtime,

unless the flowers are blue or violet,
or the plant also bears fruit
smaller than a half thimble in size.
